title: delphinium carolinianum subsp. vimineum
intro: Delphinium carolinianum subsp. vimineum, also known as Carolina larkspur or climbing larkspur, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant contains delphinine, a toxic alkaloid with medicinal properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Delphinium carolinianum subsp. vimineum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, but due to its toxicity, proper dosage and caution are essential when using this plant for medicinal purposes.
